data.narrative:
Arena AI is a Series B defense and security company founded by Pratap Ranade and Engin Ural that has raised $62M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 8 private-market peers in Defense & Security at the same stage, supplemented by 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, which applies a typical 1.9x step-up multiplier from Series B forward momentum to the $62M raised proxy, yields $118M. Peer-median analysis across 8 same-sector, same-stage private companies—including Vannevar Labs ($1.5B), Dream Security ($1.5B), Harmattan AI ($1.4B), Code Metal ($1.2B), and Castelion ($1.0B)—produces a median implied valuation of $1.1B, with a range spanning $300M to $1.5B.

The triangulated valuation range is $100M (low) to $1.3B (high), with a median of $621M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty in early-stage defense-tech valuations, where funding velocity and customer traction vary significantly across the peer set. The stage-progression floor ($118M) assumes minimal premium beyond capital deployed; the peer median substantially exceeds this, suggesting that operational and revenue metrics likely carry outsized weight in current market pricing. Key uncertainties include Arena AI's revenue run-rate, customer concentration, and technical differentiation relative to the peer cohort.
